project.name: model_architecture
project.description: Clean/onion architecture and CQRS reference implementation showing how stable domain and application layers can be exposed through replaceable HTTP and GraphQL transports, with persistence behind narrow ports. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.name: Domain Core
layers.description: Business entities, rules, ports, and typed domain errors that define the stable library domain.

layers.name: Application CQRS
layers.description: Commands, queries, application ports, and use-case orchestration that coordinate domain behavior.

layers.name: Persistence and Data
layers.description: SQL repositories, SQL command/query files, SQLx metadata, seed scripts, and database schema assets.

layers.name: HTTP Transport
layers.description: Axum HTTP server configuration, routing, request/response schemas, handlers, and HTTP error mapping.

layers.name: GraphQL Transport
layers.description: GraphQL server configuration, schema modules, resolvers, routing, and GraphQL-specific request handling.

layers.name: Authentication Support
layers.description: Reusable authentication primitives, JWT handling, claims, errors, and auth ports shared by transports.

layers.name: Project Guidance and Config
layers.description: Repository-level documentation, agent guidance, workspace configuration, and Understand Anything graph metadata.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand the clean/onion architecture goal and the inward dependency rule that shapes the whole repository.

tour.title: Domain Core
tour.description: Read the domain crate root and member logic to see where reusable business rules, guards, entities, and typed domain errors live.

tour.title: Application Use Cases
tour.description: Move to application commands and queries to see how CQRS use cases orchestrate domain behavior through narrow ports.

tour.title: Persistence Adapter
tour.description: Inspect persistence repositories and SQL assets to see how adapter code implements domain/application ports without changing core logic.

tour.title: HTTP Transport
tour.description: Follow the Axum HTTP entry point, router, and representative handlers to see request parsing, command/query calls, response DTO mapping, and error translation.

tour.title: GraphQL Transport
tour.description: Compare the GraphQL entry point and resolver modules with the HTTP transport to see how both expose the same internal use cases through separate adapter code.

tour.title: Authentication Support
tour.description: Review the auth core crate to understand shared JWT claims, verification ports, and auth errors used by transport adapters.

tour.title: Project Rules for Future Changes
tour.description: Finish with the agent and project skill guidance that captures the architectural constraints reviewers should enforce.
